Title: T0.1 Repo bootstrap and standards
Body:
## Goal
Establish project skeleton, conventions, and local dev entrypoints for the microservices repo.

## Deliverables
- Monorepo structure: /cmd, /internal, /proto, /deploy, /docs
- Makefile targets: up, down, test, lint, demo
- Initial docker-compose.yml placeholder (services stubbed)
- Go module init + pinned tool versions (golangci-lint, buf/protoc)
- Conventions doc (errors, context usage, logging format)

## Acceptance Criteria
- `make test` runs successfully (even if only skeleton tests)
- `make lint` runs and passes
- Repo builds on a clean machine with documented prerequisites

Title: T0.2 Observability baseline shared package
Body:
## Goal
Standardize structured logging and Prometheus metrics across all services.

## Dependencies
- T0.1 Repo bootstrap and standards

## Deliverables
- Shared package: /internal/platform/observability
- Structured logger (zap or zerolog) with consistent fields
- HTTP /metrics endpoint per service (Prometheus format)
- Common metric labels: service, method, status

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each service exposes /metrics
- Request count + latency metrics recorded for gRPC and HTTP gateway
- Logs include request correlation identifiers where applicable

Title: T0.3 gRPC platform baseline (server/client)
Body:
## Goal
Provide shared gRPC bootstrap with interceptors for logging, metrics, and panic recovery.

## Dependencies
- T0.1 Repo bootstrap and standards
- T0.2 Observability baseline shared package

## Deliverables
- Shared package: /internal/platform/grpc
- Unary interceptor chain: logging, metrics, panic recovery
- Client defaults: per-call deadlines/timeouts, keepalive (optional)
- Enable server reflection for local debugging (grpcurl/evans)

## Acceptance Criteria
- New services can adopt the shared gRPC bootstrap with minimal boilerplate
- Interceptor metrics visible in Prometheus and logs
- grpcurl can list services locally via reflection

Title: T1.1 Ledger v1 domain model + invariants (unit tests first)
Body:
## Goal
Define the ledger domain model and enforce core accounting invariants for a single asset (e.g., BTC satoshis).

## Dependencies
- T0.1 Repo bootstrap and standards

## Deliverables
- Domain types: Posting, Transaction, AccountID, Asset, Amount (int64 satoshis)
- Validation rules: balanced postings (sum==0), non-zero amounts, consistent asset, no overdraft on user accounts
- Table-driven unit tests for invariants
- Optional: Go fuzz tests for balancing/validation

## Acceptance Criteria
- Unbalanced transactions are rejected with clear error
- Overdraft attempts are rejected for user accounts
- Unit tests cover at least: balanced/unbalanced, invalid amounts, overdraft, asset mismatch

Title: T1.2 Ledger MySQL schema + migrations
Body:
## Goal
Create the MySQL schema and migrations for an append-only ledger with idempotency.

## Dependencies
- T0.1 Repo bootstrap and standards

## Deliverables
- Migration tool configured (goose or golang-migrate)
- Tables: ledger_transactions (tx_id, idempotency_key unique, reference, created_at, status)
- Tables: ledger_entries (entry_id, tx_id FK, account_id, amount signed int64, asset, created_at)
- Optional materialized balances table: ledger_balances (account_id, asset, balance) with indexes
- Indexes for account/time queries and idempotency uniqueness

## Acceptance Criteria
- Migrations run cleanly against local MySQL
- Unique idempotency key enforced at DB level
- Entries can be queried efficiently by account_id and created_at

Title: T1.3 Ledger repository (MySQL) + integration tests
Body:
## Goal
Implement atomic posting and safe balance enforcement with real MySQL integration tests.

## Dependencies
- T1.1 Ledger v1 domain model + invariants (unit tests first)
- T1.2 Ledger MySQL schema + migrations

## Deliverables
- Repository interface + MySQL implementation
- Atomic DB transaction: insert transaction + entries (+ update balances if materialized)
- Overdraft prevention using row locks (SELECT ... FOR UPDATE) where applicable
- Integration tests using MySQL container (docker-compose): idempotency replay, atomic rollback, concurrency safety

## Acceptance Criteria
- Posting is atomic: partial failures do not persist any entries
- Idempotency: repeated same key returns same tx outcome without double effects
- Concurrency test: two concurrent debits cannot create negative balance

Title: T1.4 Ledger gRPC API (proto) + server implementation
Body:
## Goal
Expose ledger operations via gRPC with correct status codes and validation.

## Dependencies
- T0.3 gRPC platform baseline (server/client)
- T1.3 Ledger repository (MySQL) + integration tests

## Deliverables
- Protobuf service: PostTransaction, GetBalance, ListEntries (pagination)
- Error mapping: InvalidArgument (malformed), FailedPrecondition (insufficient funds), AlreadyExists (idempotency conflict)
- Server implementation using domain validation + repository
- Enable reflection for grpcurl demos

## Acceptance Criteria
- PostTransaction rejects invalid postings and enforces overdraft rules
- GetBalance reflects latest committed postings
- ListEntries returns stable paginated results

Title: T1.5 Ledger health + metrics hardening
Body:
## Goal
Make the ledger service operable with health checks and key metrics.

## Dependencies
- T1.4 Ledger gRPC API (proto) + server implementation
- T0.2 Observability baseline shared package

## Deliverables
- gRPC health service enabled
- Readiness/liveness endpoints (or gRPC health probes in k8s)
- Metrics: request latency/counts, posted tx count, idempotency replay count, DB error count

## Acceptance Criteria
- Kubernetes can use readiness/liveness probes successfully
- Prometheus scrapes ledger metrics and dashboards can display core signals
- Logs include tx_id and idempotency_key for write operations

Title: T2.1 Account service schema + repository
Body:
## Goal
Implement minimal user storage and mapping to ledger account IDs.

## Dependencies
- T0.1 Repo bootstrap and standards

## Deliverables
- MySQL tables: users (user_id, email, created_at)
- Optional: user_accounts (user_id, asset, ledger_account_id)
- Repository with unit tests and basic integration test

## Acceptance Criteria
- CreateUser persists user and returns stable user_id
- GetUser returns correct record and handles not-found cleanly

Title: T2.2 Account gRPC API + implementation
Body:
## Goal
Expose minimal identity operations for other services.

## Dependencies
- T0.3 gRPC platform baseline (server/client)
- T2.1 Account service schema + repository

## Deliverables
- Protobuf service: CreateUser, GetUser, GetLedgerAccount (v1 returns `user:<id>` for BTC)
- gRPC server implementation using repository
- Input validation (email format basic), error mapping

## Acceptance Criteria
- Wallet/gateway can create and retrieve users via gRPC
- Ledger account mapping is deterministic and documented

Title: T3.1 Deposit workflow (wallet orchestrator)
Body:
## Goal
Implement deposit orchestration that credits a user via the ledger using idempotent references.

## Dependencies
- T1.4 Ledger gRPC API (proto) + server implementation
- T2.2 Account gRPC API + implementation

## Deliverables
- Wallet gRPC API: Deposit(user_id, amount, reference, idempotency_key)
- Translate to ledger PostTransaction postings: external -amount, user:<id> +amount
- Pass-through idempotency to ledger and return tx_id

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deposit increases user balance
- Retry with same idempotency key does not double-credit
- Invalid user or invalid amount returns clear error

Title: T3.2 Withdraw reservation + finalize workflow
Body:
## Goal
Implement a two-step withdrawal flow (reserve then finalize) with correct ledger postings and failure handling.

## Dependencies
- T3.1 Deposit workflow (wallet orchestrator)
- T1.3 Ledger repository (MySQL) + integration tests

## Deliverables
- Wallet gRPC API: InitiateWithdrawal(user_id, amount, reference, idempotency_key) -> withdrawal_id
- Wallet gRPC API: FinalizeWithdrawal(withdrawal_id, result) (success/fail)
- MySQL table: withdrawals (id, user_id, amount, state, reference, created_at, updated_at)
- Initiate postings: user:<id> -amount, custody:pending +amount
- Finalize success postings: custody:pending -amount, external +amount
- Finalize failure postings: custody:pending -amount, user:<id> +amount

## Acceptance Criteria
- Initiate decreases user available balance immediately (reservation semantics)
- Finalize success clears pending and leaves user debited
- Finalize failure releases funds back to user
- Flow is idempotent at each stage (no double-finalize effects)

Title: T3.3 Wallet observability + failure simulation
Body:
## Goal
Add operational signals and controlled failure simulation to demonstrate reliability thinking.

## Dependencies
- T3.2 Withdraw reservation + finalize workflow
- T0.2 Observability baseline shared package

## Deliverables
- Configurable failure injection for finalize step (simulate broadcast failure)
- Metrics: withdrawals initiated/finalized, finalize failures, retries, timeouts
- Structured logs include withdrawal_id, reference, ledger tx_id

## Acceptance Criteria
- Demo can show failure then recovery without balance corruption
- Metrics reflect failure counts and success rates

Title: T4.1 HTTP Gateway REST API wired to gRPC services
Body:
## Goal
Expose a human-friendly REST interface (curl/Postman) that calls internal gRPC services.

## Dependencies
- T2.2 Account gRPC API + implementation
- T3.2 Withdraw reservation + finalize workflow
- T0.2 Observability baseline shared package

## Deliverables
- HTTP server (net/http) with request logging + metrics
- Endpoints: POST /users, POST /deposit, POST /withdraw/initiate, POST /withdraw/finalize, GET /balances/:userId, GET /ledger/:userId/entries
- Input validation and error translation
- gRPC clients to Account, Wallet, Ledger with deadlines

## Acceptance Criteria
- All key flows runnable via curl
- Gateway returns useful HTTP status codes and messages
- Gateway timeouts and retries do not cause double effects (idempotency keys used)

Title: T4.2 OpenAPI + optional Swagger UI + Postman collection
Body:
## Goal
Make the demo frictionless for reviewers with API docs and ready-made requests.

## Dependencies
- T4.1 HTTP Gateway REST API wired to gRPC services

## Deliverables
- OpenAPI spec for gateway endpoints (handwritten acceptable)
- Optional Swagger UI container in docker-compose
- Postman collection in /docs/postman.json

## Acceptance Criteria
- Reviewer can import Postman collection and run through flows quickly
- OpenAPI spec matches deployed gateway behavior

Title: T5.1 docker-compose full stack (services + observability)
Body:
## Goal
Provide a one-command local runtime for the entire system.

## Dependencies
- T1.5 Ledger health + metrics hardening
- T3.3 Wallet observability + failure simulation
- T4.1 HTTP Gateway REST API wired to gRPC services

## Deliverables
- docker-compose services: mysql, (redis optional), ledger, account, wallet, gateway, prometheus, grafana
- Stable ports and environment variables
- Network config and service health checks

## Acceptance Criteria
- `make up` brings the stack up cleanly
- Prometheus scrapes all services and Grafana can connect
- `make down` cleans up volumes optionally

Title: T5.2 Demo scenario script (make demo)
Body:
## Goal
Provide a deterministic scripted walkthrough to prove correctness (idempotency + reservations) without a UI.

## Dependencies
- T5.1 docker-compose full stack (services + observability)

## Deliverables
- scripts/demo.sh or Go-based runner that calls gateway endpoints
- Scenario: create two users, deposit, transfer (optional), initiate withdrawal, finalize success and failure, print balances and sample entries
- Include explicit idempotency retry step to show no double-credit/debit

## Acceptance Criteria
- `make demo` completes successfully on a clean machine
- Output is readable and clearly shows state transitions
- Demonstrates at least one failure case and recovery

Title: T6.1 Kubernetes manifests (local cluster compatible)
Body:
## Goal
Package the services for Kubernetes (EKS-style) and validate on a local cluster (kind/minikube).

## Dependencies
- T1.5 Ledger health + metrics hardening
- T4.1 HTTP Gateway REST API wired to gRPC services

## Deliverables
- k8s manifests: Deployments + Services for each component
- ConfigMaps/Secrets examples
- Readiness/liveness probes
- Optional HPA for gateway and ledger

## Acceptance Criteria
- `kubectl apply -f deploy/k8s` works on kind/minikube
- Pods become Ready and services routable
- Health probes behave correctly under restarts

Title: T7.1 Architecture docs + runbook + ADRs
Body:
## Goal
Document architecture, invariants, and operating procedures in a reviewer-friendly format.

## Dependencies
- T5.2 Demo scenario script (make demo)

## Deliverables
- docs/architecture.md (service boundaries, data flows, sequence diagrams optional)
- docs/ledger.md (invariants, idempotency, reservation model)
- docs/runbook.md (how to run, debug, metrics, common failures)
- docs/adrs/*.md (integer amounts, idempotency design, reservations)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Reviewer can run the system and understand design tradeoffs quickly
- Docs describe how correctness is ensured and how to observe issues
